text:
Market today: The market mood is cautious, driven by Fed interest rate discussions and mixed signals in the chip sector. Strong AI chip demand is noted, but some chip names are struggling, hinting at underlying profit-taking.

Your holdings: SOXL (-40.12%) and SPAL (-38.97%) need immediate attention. Both are leveraged ETFs with deep drawdowns, amplifying risk on margin. SMH (-9.38%) and QQQI (-1.72%) are also flagged for trimming.

Watch: Monitor Fed commentary for interest rate shifts and observe how the chip sector reacts to strong AI demand amidst recent share price drops.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk in your leveraged positions while carefully watching tech sector sentiment.
plays: SOXL: Consider trimming this leveraged position given its SELL status and -40.12% loss. Re-evaluate your risk tolerance here.
SPAL: Review this new leveraged ETF with a -38.97% loss. Its fast moves and decay amplify risk on margin.
NVDA: The scanner shows a BUY setup at 206.8-206.6 with a stop at 205.71, potentially benefiting from strong AI chip demand.
